message:
Stage 0.5 (typed) D0.R escalated on round 1 (before cap) — the directed revise cannot fix the findings in place: Cannot resolve every D0.5 finding by an in-place core edit because thm:sharp-frontier has statement-level defects owned by the orchestrator/proto, not D0.R. Finding `missing_theorem_assumption`: the theorem statement omits `ass:two-block-homophily` even though the proof and support classification use `m>=2` and `a>b>0`; fixing this requires changing the theorem statement/premises. Finding `ill_typed_frontier_parameterization`: the theorem statement treats `alpha`, `mu_S`, `V_S`, `lambda_S`, and the active cells as fixed-`kappa` objects, but these quantities vary with `r`; correcting this requires restating the frontier parameterization in the theorem statement. Under the hard limit, statement/conclusion edits are discarded on re-solve, so this round must escalate to the orchestrator.
Open findings: missing_theorem_assumption@thm:sharp-frontier, ill_typed_frontier_parameterization@thm:sharp-frontier, positioning_gap@thm:sharp-rho-star, citation_relevance_gap@thm:sharp-frontier. Provide guidance via the D0 directive (a new direction / a paper to adapt / a reframing) and re-run, or rewind D0/D-1.2.

message:
Stage 0.5 (typed) D0.R escalated on round 0 (before cap) — the directed revise cannot fix the findings in place: Escalation required for thm:sharp-rho-star. Finding closed_form_overclaim is statement-level: the asserted sentence 'rho_star is the closed-form value obtained from the seven relaxed active-set cases together with these truncation-segment endpoint/interior cases' needs restating to separate the kappa=0 exposed-face branch from the kappa>0 seven-support computation. Finding unsupported_specialization is also statement-level: the asserted recovery of the odd-m gap window needs narrowing to the additional low-scale/oddness regime of thm:gap-window, or else a new derivation from this theorem's own hypotheses. Because both fixes require changing the theorem's asserted text/conclusion rather than only proof/prose/wiring, they belong to the orchestrator proto re-solve, not an in-place core edit.
